articles.title: Odisha sizzles under severe heatwave; warnings issued for 6 districts - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha continues to reel under an intense heatwave, with soaring temperatures making conditions increasingly unbearable across the state. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning for six districts, Khordha, Keonjhar, Angul, Sambalpur, Jharsuguda, and Bolangir, for Friday, cautioning residents against prolonged exposure to extreme heat.
In addition, a yellow warning has been issued for 17 districts due to hot and humid conditions, further compounding discomfort levels. The weather office has also sounded alerts for thunderstorms and rainfall in six districts, indicating a possible shift in weather patterns in the coming days.
On Thursday, Jharsuguda and Talcher emerged as the hottest places in the state, with temperatures soaring to a blistering 44°C. As many as 24 cities recorded temperatures above 40°C, underscoring the widespread intensity of the heatwave.
The extreme weather has disrupted daily life, with people avoiding outdoor activities during peak hours. Health experts have advised adequate hydration and precautionary measures, especially for vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly, and outdoor workers.
However, there may be some relief on the horizon. The IMD has forecast increased thunderstorm and rainfall activity from April 26, which could bring down temperatures in several parts of the state.

Authorities remain on alert, urging citizens to follow advisories and take necessary precautions as Odisha battles one of the season’s harshest heat spells.

articles.title: Odisha sizzles as 24 places cross 40 degree celsius; heatwave alert till April 25 - The New Indian Express
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
BHUBANESWAR: Odisha remained in the grip of intense heat on Thursday with 24 places in the state recording 40 degree Celsius or more. The Bhubaneswar met centre has forecast hot and humid weather as well as warm nights in parts of the state till Saturday.
On the day, Jharsuguda and Talcher recorded the highest maximum day temperature of 44 deg C each. They were followed by Titilagarh which logged 43.8 deg C, Dhenkanal and Angul 43.5 deg C each, Hirakud 43.4 deg C, and Sambalpur and Paralakhemundi 43 deg C each.
Twin City, meanwhile, also recorded a surge in mercury with Bhubaneswar reading 42.1 deg C and Cuttack 41.8 deg C, the highest in both so far this season.
The regional met office has forecast possibility of heatwave at isolated places in Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, Balangir, Keonjhar, Angul and Khurda districts on Friday. The hot and humid weather may prevail at a few places in Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati districts during the period.
People in the coastal regions can, however, expect some respite from the punishing heat Saturday onwards as the weather body has forecast a dip in mercury in these areas.
In its extended range outlook issued on the day, the met office said the maximum temperature is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the state from April 24 to 30 and normal to above normal over interior districts between May 1 and 7.
It also said that there is low probability of cyclogenesis over south Bay of Bengal in the first week of May. Weather experts said they are closely monitoring the atmospheric conditions.
As it is, the Bay of Bengal region is notorious for being one of the most cyclone-prone areas in the world. The relatively warmer sea surface temperatures in the Bay, particularly from April to November, fuel the intensity of cyclones, making them more frequent and stronger in this region, weather experts said.
Odisha is highly vulnerable to severe tropical cyclones in May, a month that marks the pre-monsoon season, and often produces intense storms due to high sea surface temperatures in the Bay of Bengal. The tropical storms which hit the state in May include Fani (2019) and Yaas (2021).

articles.title: Odisha Heatwave Curfew! Yellow Warning For 6 Districts; Hot Night Alert In Balangir! - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
The heatwave in Odisha has reached dangerous levels as Jharsuguda and Talcher recorded a scorching 44°C. According to the Regional Meteorological Centre, the situation is set to worsen today, with coastal Odisha expected to cross the 45°C mark. A Yellow Warning has been issued for Khordha, Sambalpur, Balangir, Keonjhar, Angul, and Jharsuguda. Shockingly, even the nights are becoming unbearable, with Balangir, Sambalpur, and Kandhamal under a "Warm Night" alert. Experts warn that there will be no relief from this intense heat for at least the next two weeks. Stay indoors, stay hydrated, and watch our full report for the latest district-wise temperature updates and IMD guidelines.
